item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The images that were used in this article are non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as the first image could not be sourced, assumed non-compliant, no source found indicates the second image has been released under Creative Commons, and the third image comes from here with no indication it has been released under Creative Commons. Thus, the images have been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Incident 1969-A, Is just me or when you hear that many civilians get terminated while dancing your mind flickers to “Murder on the dance floor” . Or even “You better not kill the groove”.

+1 :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love how the addendum perfectly contrasts the whole article before it. What turned out to be a potentially world-ending sphere was originally marketed as a relatively mundane automatic disco ball.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What if it eventually developed a reverse-Sayaw zone and shrouded the world in perfect darkness? Best to keep it relatively still.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So… why don't we let it remain in a Beta state?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very well written. Nice and scary, I love the SCP's we have to tiptoe around. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Site. I took out the references to Area. Thanks for catching that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
it is located at the recovery site (now named Site 1969)
spanning the entirety of Site-1969
Along the edge of Site-1969

but

…the research and administrative staff of Area 1969
Area Director Nelson

So, is it Site or Area?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Disco didn't die, it was given Keter duty. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't get it, neutral vote for me right now

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
An extremely inventive keter SCP. From the title, 'Disco Inferno', I was concerned that I was about to read a cringe-worthy article concerning a nightclub that was actually a portal to hell. Thankfully, I was pleasantly surprised. +1

A couple of minor issues I have with the article:

50 small transmitters are attached to the walls

The use of 'small' seems totally superfluous. Why does it matter that the transmitters used are smaller than the average transmitting device?

In the event that more than two of these transmitters stop working

The phrase 'stop working' is rather non-scientific. In this context wouldn't the word 'malfunction' function better?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I didn't find this difficult to read or boring in the slightest.

Amazing job, Reject, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hard to read, too boring to bother with. Downvoted

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A) Bullets do the job faster and more reliably, I think. Or, for that matter, poison gas, if they had that on hand.
B) If they didn't have enough non-lethal weaponry to stop a thousand people from moving without killing them on-hand, the Sayaw zone could potentially grow out of their ability to have a hope of handling it if they didn't stop it pronto.

This is just my speculation, mind you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So it's easier to kill large numbers of civilians (with all the associated paperwork) than it is to just use rubber bullets, water hoses, mustard gas, etc. to rout them?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They were dancing and making the Sayaw zone expand. That couldn't be allowed to continue.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The site generally uses this definition of terminate.

Why did the Foundation order the death of 1,053 civilians?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Terminated is used correctly, though if the consensus is that I should change that detail, I will.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have the same irk. Either the foundation went really far into overkill mode, or the word "terminated" is being improperly used.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ahh, that's what I was missing, thank you!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The Lambda State is artificial, fine-tuned by the Foundation to keep SCP-1969 easily contained. If the pistons stop working properly, it will go into an Alpha state.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's what it is, I got that part. What i don't got is what makes it go from [state X] to Lambda, or from Lambda to [state X].

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The Lambda state constitutes constant fluctuation between Alpha and Beta states, to the effect that the Sayaw Zone's size never changes significantly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not getting what causes or ends a Lambda state.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's an interesting idea. Let me ponder that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The MC&D connection seems a little odd, to me. I know it says it hasn't been tested, but they are trying to run a business. Products that wipe out large numbers of potential customers are notably bad for business. Given that they're a successful company, they can afford to be somewhat cautious, so I can't see them making a mistake like that. I think what would cinch the upvote for me would be an addendum, stating that a raid on a MC&D data store containing regional accounting information for the time period turned up no record of the object or its alleged sale, and that investigations are ongoing into who actually held the auction.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: how…romantic c.c

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's 173's girlfriend.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What IS that :O

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes, with respect to the ceiling. Fix'd.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So since they reflect normally and penetrate, does that mean that the photons themselves multiply every time they strike an opaque object? Or is it just magic?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah, so it is. My apologies.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You say it turns clockwise… with respect to what? The ceiling?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Are these spins relative to being viewed from above or below? Something like that might be relevant.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Tayoy magsayawan! Clever naming. :D

I think this could use just a smidge of editing for clarity and brevity, but I highly approve of the Keter disco ball. I think that's half of what I enjoy about Keter articles; you look at the object class, look back to the picture and go, "A Keter blank? Oh boy, here we go!"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes, that's exactly what that says. The photons penetrate - but at the same time, they seem to act normally as well. How? Well, that's an SCP object for you, isn't it? Dern things just don't care about trivia like physics or causality…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Basically what they'er saying is it somehow seems to penetrate AND reflect/absorb, I think.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
penetrate == pass through.
My point stands.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Someone mentioned this in chat a while ago and I nearly shit bricks, because it's a very good point. However, since they mentioned it in chat, I added a sentence.

Despite this, all electromagnetic waves seem to reflect normally, but paradoxically seem to penetrate materials as well without lowering in intensity.

There used to be a thing in there about using it as a power source (in the first revision, I think), but that would obviously be a bad idea considering how dangerous mining Keter level objects for power is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The Lambda state constitutes constant fluctuation between Alpha and Beta states, to the effect that the Sayaw Zone's size never changes significantly.

Final paragraph before the addendum.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wait.. if the emissions within Sayaw zone pass through pretty much anything, they'd pass through people as well, wouldn't they? Meaning there's a large zone of utter darkness around the thing as our perception relies on reflection and absorbtion of photons.
Rather than radiation poisoning, if spread over the earth's surface it would cause utter darkness, photosynthesizing lifeforms to starve and as such pretty much a Dead Greenhouse scenario - i think the only still working stuff would be chemosynthesis based lifecycles down near the ocean floor where gases leak from the crust.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice, although I was confused about the Lambda state.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: MC&D seem like a really odd choice for this one. Also, you never explicitly define the Lambda state.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not seeing how this thing could cause radiation poisoning, since it says that the Sayaw Zone affects light emanating from the ball, plus ionizing radiation doesn't bounce or reflect (except maybe for hard ultra-violet). If you put in the Zone a box who's insides were lined with mirrors then the light endlessly bouncing back and forth inside would generate light, with the intensity increasing over time, but by the time the intensity was enough to cause damage to the surroundings it would also cause damage to the mirrors, stopping the process (though it could easily permanently blind anyone in the area). So I just don't see how this could be Keter.

Also:

Approximately ██ seconds after the cumulative movement inside of the Sayaw Zone reduces to ██%,

I think that this is missing something, and should read something like "reduces to ██% of the previous Alpha state threshold",

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pretty good, just one thing felt a bit wrong, this bit: 1,053 civilians terminated. I suggest changing to killed or redact the number of people killed, it's nothing big but i was recently reading the guides and this just keeps bugging me, but anyway, really good. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Disco inferno, etc, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I get it, 1969

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Confusing, but good. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good call.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very creative, and I always appreciate a good keter. Took a second read through to figure out exactly what it was saying but now it makes good sense. I would remove the bit about it being used for a power source, it detracts from the article. Overall good work, upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm kind of sad that my lurker swag led me to reading this earlier on the sandbox, keeping me from being able to enjoy this fresh now. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Burn baby burn!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You finally posted it, so I can finally upvote it. +1
